Info
About

Perfusion Tools – CPB & ECMO Perfusion Calculators

Transparent CPB and ECMO calculators for perfusion planning, indexed flow, heparin dosing, hemodilution, and clinical unit conversion.

Start here

Choose a calculator below. Each tool opens on its own dedicated page with the full workflow, formulas, methodology, and clinical notes.

Calculator
BSA Calculator

Estimate body surface area with common formulas for CPB flow indexing and perfusion planning.

→
Calculator
Lean Body Mass Calculator

Calculate LBM and lean-indexed body-size references used in selected perfusion and dosing workflows.

→
Calculator
DO2i / GDP Calculator

Calculate indexed oxygen delivery and required flow targets for goal-directed perfusion protocols.

→
Calculator
Heparin Calculator

Review heparin bolus dosing support for adult CPB with resistance-aware adjustments.

→
Calculator
Predicted Hematocrit Calculator

Estimate post-prime dilutional hematocrit to support transfusion and hemodilution planning.

→
Calculator
Z-score Calculator

Calculate pediatric echocardiography Z-scores for selected cardiac dimensions using published references.

→
Calculator
Priming Volume Calculator

Estimate tubing segment prime contribution using inner diameter and length inputs.

→
Calculator
Time Calculator

Calculate elapsed time, cross-clamp time, bypass time, and case intervals for operative documentation.

→
Reference
Quick Reference

Access concise CPB and ECMO reference tables for quick clinical review.

→
Calculator
Unit Converter

Convert common perfusion units for blood gas, pressure, flow, cannula sizing, and clinical documentation.

→

Privacy and safety note

Calculators run in-browser on their standalone pages. They are intended for clinical calculation support and do not replace clinician judgment, institutional protocols, or device-specific instructions.

About Perfusion Tools

About Perfusion Tools

Perfusion Tools is a browser-based project that supports day-to-day perfusion workflows with fast, transparent bedside calculations and clear reference guidance.

What this project is

A focused clinical support site for perfusion practice in CPB and ECMO environments. The goal is to reduce calculation friction and provide consistent references during real clinical workflows.

Who it is for

Perfusionists, clinicians, and educational users who need quick, structured bedside calculation support.

Why it was built

To improve speed, reduce repeated manual steps, and support more consistent communication and decision support across perfusion teams.

Design principles

Practical: Fast inputs and outputs for bedside use.

Transparent: Visible formulas and assumptions.

Consistent: Standardized outputs for team alignment.

Clinician-centered: Built around real perfusion workflow needs.

Important limitations

  • For trained clinicians and educational-reference use.
  • Not a medical device.
  • Not a substitute for institutional protocols.
  • Not a substitute for clinical judgment or direct patient monitoring.

Explore tools

DO₂i / GDP Calculator BSA Calculator Predicted Hct Pediatric Z-score

Questions or Feedback

If you have suggestions, workflow feedback, or bug reports, please contact Perfusion Tools directly.

Contact

Privacy Policy

This site is a static calculator that runs entirely in your browser. No clinical inputs, identifiers, or session data are sent to any server.

Anonymous, aggregate analytics may be used to understand feature usage (page views, button clicks) without logging IP addresses or patient information.

Inputs remain on-device and disappear when you refresh or close the page. Do not store or print screenshots that contain protected health information (PHI) on shared systems.

If you have privacy concerns or wish to report a data issue, please reach out via the contact form below. We will respond promptly with remediation steps.

Terms of Use

This calculator is provided for educational use by healthcare professionals. It is not a medical device and does not replace clinical judgment or institutional protocols.

Always confirm calculations manually, correlate with patient data, and follow attending physician orders. Use of this tool is at your own risk.

No warranty is provided, express or implied. The developers are not liable for decisions or outcomes arising from use of the calculator.

By continuing to use this site, you agree to these terms. If you do not agree, please discontinue use.

Contact

We value your feedback. For feature requests or bug reports, please contact us:

perfusiontools@gmail.com
Copied!

© Perfusion Tools. Educational use for healthcare professionals.
